title: feat: show accurate tiktoken output count in shell tool footers

similarOrDuplicatePRs.title: fix(core): truncate workspace tool output at token level instead of line boundaries
feat(workspace): ANSI stripping, tail pipe extraction, and tiktoken-based output limits for workspace tools

summary: Refactors output-helpers (applyTokenLimit, applyTokenLimitSandwich, truncateOutput) to return a TruncationResult object ({ text, tokens }) instead of a plain string, reusing the tiktoken count already computed during truncation. The accurate token count is emitted in the data-sandbox-exit chunk from execute-command, get-process-output, and kill-process tools. The mc TUI and playground UI display the token count in the tool footer when it exceeds 1k tokens. All callers of the changed functions (grep, list-files, read-file) are updated to use .text from the new return type. Existing tests are updated with 62 assertions passing locally.
maintainerNotes: PR is still in draft status. CI has 3 failures: Core Package Tests, Full Test Suite, and Lint. The Lint failure and Core test failures need investigation — they may or may not be related to this PR's changes. The code change is well-structured: it's a natural extension of the existing tiktoken truncation infrastructure (built in prior PRs #13440 and #13828 by the same author). The return type refactor is clean — all callers are updated. The playground-ui peer dep on @mastra/core was bumped from >=1.7.1-0 to >=1.9.1-0. No reviews have been submitted yet. Author should fix CI failures and mark as ready for review.
